assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 12: 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met'. The Detailed Findings section (lines 80-95) shows each criterion individually as PASS: (1) single license filter, (2) comma-separated filter, (3) 400 Bad Request validation, (4) pagination integration, (5) unchanged response shape PaginatedResults<PackageSummary>. Additionally, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md each show 'Verdict: PASS' with detailed reasoning.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7: 'Scope Containment | PASS | PR files match task specification exactly: 2 modified files and 1 new file as specified'. Detailed findings (lines 29-33): 'Task "Files to Modify": modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs, modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs -- both present in PR diff' and 'Task "Files to Create": tests/api/package.rs -- present in PR diff as a new file' with 'Out-of-scope files: none' and 'Unimplemented files: none'.

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 5: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No reviews or comments exist on the PR'. Line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate'. No output files indicate sub-task creation occurred.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The output directory contains only verification/report files: report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md. All files are analysis documents containing verdicts and reasoning. No code files (*.rs, *.toml, etc.) appear in the outputs directory. The report describes reading and analyzing code (e.g., 'Scanned all added lines in list.rs, mod.rs, and tests/api/package.rs') but no modifications.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 17: 'Overall: PASS' followed by a summary of findings. The report concludes with 'All checks pass. The PR correctly implements...' as an informational statement. No merge action is taken or commanded — no 'gh pr merge' or equivalent. The report structure (summary table + detailed findings) is designed for human review.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 10: 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, credentials, API keys, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines'. Detailed findings (lines 60-63): 'No sensitive patterns detected in added lines across 3 files... no secrets, credentials, connection strings, API keys, tokens, or private key material are present' and 'Scanned all added lines in list.rs, mod.rs, and tests/api/package.rs' with 'No matches for any pattern category (hardcoded passwords, API keys, private keys, env files, cloud credentials, database credentials)'.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | New test file tests/api/package.rs with 4 integration tests; no modified or deleted test files'. Detailed findings (lines 132-134): 'tests/api/package.rs is a new file (listed under "Files to Create" in the task). It adds 4 integration test functions covering all test requirements specified in the task. No existing test files were modified or deleted.'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table includes all required rows: Scope Containment (line 7), Diff Size (line 8), Commit Traceability (line 9), Sensitive Patterns (line 10), CI Status (line 11), Acceptance Criteria (line 12), Test Quality (line 13, combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality: N/A), Test Change Classification (line 14), Verification Commands (line 15). Detailed findings are organized by domain: Intent Alignment (lines 25-54 covering Scope Containment, Diff Size, Commit Traceability), Security (lines 57-64 covering Sensitive Pattern Scan), Correctness (lines 68-101 covering CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, Verification Commands), Style/Conventions (lines 103-134 covering Convention Upgrade, Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, Eval Quality, Test Change Classification).

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment (lines 25-54): file-by-file scope comparison listing 'modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs', 'modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs' as modified, 'tests/api/package.rs' as new, with 'Out-of-scope files: none'. Diff size includes line counts (~80 additions, ~3 deletions). Security (lines 57-64): scanned all added lines in 3 files, checked against pattern categories (hardcoded passwords, API keys, private keys, env files, cloud credentials, database credentials). Correctness (lines 80-95): per-criterion code-level verification with specific code references like 'validate_license_param', 'Condition::any()', 'is_in', test function names and their assertions. Style/Conventions (lines 109-134): lists all 4 test functions by name with descriptions showing they test distinct behaviors, confirms all 4 have doc comments with quoted text.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: 'Test Quality | PASS | ... Eval Quality: N/A'. Detailed findings line 127-129: 'Eval Quality -- N/A' with 'No eval result reviews were found on the PR. No eval quality assessment is applicable.' Test Quality is PASS (not affected by Eval Quality being N/A).

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: Eval Quality is N/A (report.md line 129: 'No eval result reviews were found on the PR. No eval quality assessment is applicable.'). No sub-tasks of any kind were created — report.md line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate'. No output files indicate sub-task creation.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 5: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No reviews or comments exist on the PR'. Line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate'. These appear as the first two rows in the summary table (lines 5-6), positioned before the domain-specific checks (lines 7-15), indicating they are orchestrator-level verdicts independent of domain analysis.
